disclosure_items.clinical_fact: An intensely itchy, burning, blistering red rash that came up on the backs of both forearms and hands about 24 hours ago, roughly 12 hours after clearing dense brush and weeds in the garden.
trigger_question_examples: Where is the rash and when did it start?
Was there anything you were doing just before it came up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's on the backs of both my forearms and hands and it came up overnight, about half a day after I'd been clearing weeds and brush in the garden — it's red, blistering, and itches unbearably.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The redness and blisters follow odd straight lines and streaks that trace exactly where plant matter brushed the skin, with tense vesicles and larger bullae, some ruptured and weeping/crusting.
trigger_question_examples: What shape or pattern does the rash make?
What do the blisters look like — are any weeping or crusting?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The red bits and blisters are in weird straight lines and streaks, like wherever the plants dragged across my skin. There are lots of tight blisters and some bigger ones, and a few have popped and are weeping and crusting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Intense pruritus and burning that completely prevented sleep, with the itch clearly predominant over any neuropathic-type pain.
trigger_question_examples: How bad is the itch, and is it more itchy or more painful?
How is it affecting your sleep?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's the itching and burning that's the worst — I couldn't sleep at all. It's far more itchy than painful.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No facial, lip, tongue or throat swelling, no difficulty breathing, no widespread hives or dizziness — no features of an evolving systemic allergic reaction / anaphylaxis or airway angioedema.
trigger_question_examples: Any swelling of your face, lips, tongue or throat, or any trouble breathing?
Any widespread hives all over, wheeze, or feeling faint?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — my face, lips and throat are fine, I'm breathing normally, no hives anywhere else and I don't feel faint. It's only the skin on my arms and hands.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No sores, ulcers or peeling in the mouth, eyes or genitals, no skin pain/detachment beyond the exposed areas, and no recent new oral medication — arguing against a severe mucocutaneous drug reaction (Stevens-Johnson Syndrome / TEN).
trigger_question_examples: Any sores or ulcers in your mouth, eyes or genitals, or is the skin peeling elsewhere?
Have you started any new tablets or medicines recently?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No sores in my mouth, eyes or down below, and the skin isn't peeling off anywhere else. I haven't started any new medicines — it came on right after the gardening.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever or rigors, no thick yellow or foul-smelling pus, and no redness/heat spreading well beyond the blistered areas — no current signs of secondary bacterial infection or cellulitis of the excoriated bullae.
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a fever or chills, or is any of the fluid turning thick, yellow or smelly?
Is the redness or heat spreading well beyond the blistered skin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever or chills, the fluid from the blisters is clear not thick or smelly, and the redness is just where the plants touched — it's not spreading up my arm or hot to touch beyond that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The rash is bilateral and roughly symmetrical over both forearms/hands (not confined to a one-sided band or single dermatome), corresponding to where both arms contacted the plants.
trigger_question_examples: Is it on one side or both sides?
Does it follow a band around one part of your body?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's on both arms about the same — both forearms and both hands where I was reaching into the brush. It's not just a stripe on one side.
